description_long: This bungalow features a low, wide profile with a prominent, dark hip roof that slopes gently on all sides. The front facade is characterized by a set of large windows and a recessed entryway, suggesting a welcoming porch area. The exterior walls are clad in a light, textured material, likely simulating brick or siding, while the roof is finished in a darker, matte material. The rear and side views reveal a more minimal window arrangement, emphasizing privacy and simplicity. The structure sits close to the ground, typical of bungalow designs, and appears to be a single-story dwelling. The overall form is compact and symmetrical, with clean lines and a practical, suburban aesthetic.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide, dark hip roof, large front windows, and a light-textured exterior. The design is compact, symmetrical, and features a welcoming front porch area.
description_view_0: Front view showing large windows and a recessed entryway, indicating a porch.
description_view_1: Side view with minimal window presence and clear view of the hip roof's slope.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain facade and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mostly solid wall with little to no window detail.
